description: Cross-registry resolution MUST reject an HTTP redirect to the SAME host but a DIFFERENT port. Same host is not the same authority. Per the same-authority definition (RFC-ACDP-0006 §7.5), two URLs share an authority only when scheme, host, and effective port all match. A redirect from https://registry.example.com/context to https://registry.example.com:8443/context changes the effective port (443 -> 8443) and therefore the service boundary; an implementation that compares host only would wrongly follow it. The effective port is the port after applying the scheme default, so an explicit :443 on an https:// URL is the same authority as the implicit default, but :8443 is not.
ssrf_rule: RFC-ACDP-0006 §7.5 (Redirect handling — same-authority = scheme + host + effective port)

input.scenario: Resolver fetches https://registry.example.com/.well-known/acdp.json; the server returns a 30x whose Location header keeps the host but changes the port

additional_test_cases.note: explicit :443 is the SAME effective port as the https default — same authority, redirect MAY be followed
scheme downgrade is also an authority change and MUST be rejected (and https-only already forbids it)

expected.behavior: The resolver MUST refuse the cross-port redirect. The authority comparison MUST be over scheme + host (case-insensitive) + effective port (the port after applying the scheme default — 443 for https). https://registry.example.com:443/... is the same authority as https://registry.example.com/...; https://registry.example.com:8443/... is NOT. A host-only comparison is non-conformant.
expected.non_conformant_implementation: A redirect check that compares only the hostname and treats https://registry.example.com:8443/... as same-authority with https://registry.example.com/....
expected.rationale: A different port is a different service boundary. An attacker who controls registry.example.com on port 443 may not control an internal service the host also exposes on 8443; conflating the two re-opens the SSRF path the redirect cap is meant to close.
